items.title:
Nielsen isn't misleading. They track streaming usage on TV specifically (in this case, HBO Max and Discovery+).

They have another report called The Media Distributor Gauge (see below for October), and it shows WBD's share at ~5.6% of TV (including streaming + cable), but that's mostly events like MLB playoffs on cable.

The cable networks segment (assets like TNT, TBS, TLC) is not included in the Netflix merger, so not really relevant for market share discussions.

A better nuance is that Nielsen measures volume, not value. HBO Max optimizes for quality over quantity, resulting in fewer hours watched but high pricing power.
